rulings.Case Title: SHAWN-MICHAEL MATHIES VS. UNION BANK, N.A.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Protective Order Re Pltf'S Survey Of Putatuive Class Memebers
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 25, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT UNION BANK, N.A.'s Motion For Protective Order Re Plaintiff's Survey Of Putative Class Memebers. Granted in part. The request for notes and recordings regarding the survey is denied without prejudice to seeking this information through a motion to compel after service of discovery requests seeking the information. rulings.Case Title: LOUIS GAMACHE et al VS. NICK VOLENTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 25, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANT PRIME PACIFIC INVESTMENTS, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action 1, 3, 5, 6, 9, 11, and 13. These claims are to be asserted against the landlords and/or owners. The parties agree and judicially noticeable documents show that defendant is not the legal owner of the premises. There is no authority supporting plaintiff's "beneficial owner" theory of liability. The Court also rejects plaintiff's alter ego theory of liability. (Postal Instant Press, Inc. v. Kaswa Corp. (2008) 162 Cal.App.4th 1510). Sustained without leave to amend as to cause of action 7 because it is not an independent claim and it is repetitive of the negligence cause of action. Overruled as to causes of action 2, 4, 8, 10, and 12. Paragraphs 52-56 of the fourth amended complaint allege that defendant had a role in managing the construction at the property and failed to perform that task in a competent fashion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BUNZL DISTRIBUTION USA, INC., A DELAWARE VS. FRANCHISE TAX BOARD, AN AGENCY OF THE STATE OF
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Otion For Protective Order; Request
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 25, 2012, Line 1. DEFENDANT FRANCHISE TAX BOARD'S Notice Of Motion And motion For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge William Shapiro, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WENDY WESTMAN VS. ROBERT SHAPIRO DR. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 25, 2012, Line 17, DEFENDANT BRADLEY SHAPIRO, M.D.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Off calendar as moot in light of the ruling on the Motion for Leave to File a Second Amended Complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: VERA CORT VS. CARL HILSZ **CONVERTED FROM U/D TO LIMITED CIVIL ACTION PER COURT ORDER SEP-29-11**
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Vera Cort Motion To Compel Further Responses And For A Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 25, 2012, Line 7. Plaintiff Vera Cort's Motion To Compel Further Responses And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge William Shapiro, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)
